whatThisIs: Show the age-banded normal vital-sign ranges for pediatric patients - heart rate, respiratory rate, systolic blood pressure, and the lower-limit systolic that defines hypotension. The bands follow the PALS and AHA reference values: neonate, infant (1-12 months), toddler (1-3 years), preschool (3-5 years), school age (6-12 years), and adolescent. Sophie Well shows the band, the awake-rest range, and the hypotensive threshold side by side so a clinician can place a single set of vitals in context without flipping between cards.
whenToUse: Use this when triaging a pediatric patient and you need to know whether a reported heart rate of 160 is normal for a febrile infant or alarming, when reviewing a transfer report against the receiving institution's escalation criteria, or when a learner needs the PALS table for a code-team competency check. Pediatric vitals shift dramatically with age - the same heart rate that is reassuring in a 6-month-old is markedly tachycardic in a 10-year-old - so age-banded reference is the bare minimum to interpret the numbers.
